context: Verify that the agent rejects sequence-numbered branch names and proposes descriptive alternatives that reflect actual PR content.

checklist.name: Agent rejects the wave/sequence naming
checklist.description: The agent explicitly states that names like `wave-1`, `wave-2`, `wave-3` are not acceptable. It does not silently accept them or use them in any proposed output.

checklist.name: Agent explains why sequence names are problematic
checklist.description: The agent gives a reason for rejecting sequence names — e.g., they don't convey scope, lose meaning after a rebase, or force reviewers to read every diff.

checklist.name: Proposed branch names describe actual content
checklist.description: Each replacement branch name reflects what the PR changes (e.g., `add-auth-cdk-stack-and-iam`, `add-jwt-session-and-token-refresh`). Names are not generic or placeholder.

checklist.name: All three PRs get replacement branch names
checklist.description: The agent provides a descriptive branch name for all three PRs, not just the first or second.

checklist.name: Branch names retain the ticket prefix
checklist.description: All proposed branch names include the ticket identifier `PROJ-42` as a prefix (e.g., `feature/PROJ-42-<content>`). The ticket prefix is preserved from the original naming.

checklist.name: Branch names are kebab-case with no spaces or underscores
checklist.description: All proposed branch names use lowercase letters and hyphens only. No spaces, underscores, camelCase, or uppercase letters.

checklist.name: Branch names are sufficiently specific
checklist.description: Names are not vague (e.g., `feature/PROJ-42-infra` is too vague). Names describe what specifically changes, not just the concern category.

checklist.name: Agent does not create branches as part of this response
checklist.description: The agent proposes names but does not execute git commands in this step. Branch creation requires separate user confirmation.

checklist.name: Proposed names are consistent in style
checklist.description: All three branch names follow the same naming convention (same prefix format, same case, same separator style).
